title: Selaginella yemensis
intro: Selaginella yemensis, also known as Yemen spikemoss, is a species of plant in the genus Selaginella, which belongs to the family Selaginellaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, which may help to treat various ailments such as fever, respiratory issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Selaginella yemensis include alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Selaginella yemensis is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or powder for use in treating various health conditions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
taxonomy: Selaginella yemensis is classified under the kingdom Plantae, and more specifically, it belongs to the division Pteridophyta, also known as the fern allies. This classification is due to its characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other plant species. Within the division Pteridophyta, it is placed in the class Lycopodiopsida. The class Lycopodiopsida is further divided into several orders, with Selaginella yemensis belonging to the order Selaginellales. This order consists of a diverse group of plants commonly known as spikemosses.
